title: LCP Ruby Type Definition
description: Schema for custom business type metadata that bundles storage type, transforms, validations, and form hints into a reusable type. Defined in config/lcp_ruby/types/. Fields referencing a custom type inherit its transforms, validations, input_type, and renderer. Field-level settings override type-level settings. Built-in types: email (string + strip/downcase + email input + email_link renderer), phone (string + strip/normalize_phone + tel input + phone_link renderer), url (string + strip/normalize_url + url input + url_link renderer), color (string + strip/downcase + color input + color_swatch renderer).

name.description: Unique type name used to reference this type from model field definitions via type: <name>. Must be unique across all type definitions.

base_type.description: The underlying database column type. Determines the ActiveRecord attribute type, database column type, and default form input behavior.

transforms.description: Transform service keys applied to the field value on assignment via ActiveRecord normalizes. Field-level transforms are merged (union) with type-level transforms. Built-in transforms: 'strip' (remove whitespace), 'downcase' (lowercase), 'normalize_url' (prepend https://), 'normalize_phone' (strip non-digits, keep leading +). Custom transforms registered in Services::Registry.

validations.description: Default validations applied to all fields of this type. Field-level validations of the same type override (not merge with) type-level validations. Built-in type validations include allow_blank: true so they only validate when a value is present.

input_type.description: Default HTML input type for form fields of this type. Overridden by field-level or presenter-level input_type. Resolution chain: presenter field input_type > type input_type > base field type.

renderer.description: Default display renderer for show/index views. Overridden by field-level or presenter-level renderer. When omitted, the value is displayed as plain text. Must be a registered renderer name.

column_options.description: Default database column options applied when creating the column. Field-level column_options override type-level options for the same keys.

limit.description: Maximum column length in characters (for string columns) or bytes.

null.description: Whether the column allows NULL values. When false, a NOT NULL constraint is added.

precision.description: Total number of significant digits (for decimal/numeric columns).

scale.description: Number of digits after the decimal point (for decimal/numeric columns).

html_input_attrs.description: Additional HTML attributes added to form input elements for fields of this type. Useful for browser-level hints like autocomplete, placeholder patterns, or inputmode. Reserved for future view partial integration.

validation.description: A validation rule applied to field values. Corresponds to ActiveModel validation types. Type-level validations include allow_blank: true by default.

type.description: The validation type. Maps to ActiveModel validators: presence, length (min/max), numericality (gt/lt/eq), format (regex), inclusion/exclusion (allowed/disallowed values), uniqueness (DB unique check), confirmation (field_confirmation match), comparison (compare two fields), custom (custom validator class), service (delegated to registered service).

options.description: Validation options passed to the ActiveModel validator. Keys depend on the validation type: length accepts minimum/maximum/is, numericality accepts greater_than/less_than/equal_to, format accepts with (regex pattern), inclusion/exclusion accept in (array of values).

message.description: Custom error message when validation fails. Overrides the default ActiveModel message. Supports i18n interpolation.
